Body covers the core stance: favor real e2e tests (no mocks at e2e layer); stubs over mocks at unit layer; contract tests between stub and real backend; zero mocks in integration tests; behavior over implementation; deterministic factories; behavior-naming; testing pyramid as shape not ratios; performance measurements require baseline + explicit boundary + reproducible harness. grep -E "cli/internal/|docs/helix/|orchTestGitOps|fakeExecuteBeadGit|gateTestGitOps|mockExecutor|script\.go|integration_helper_test" library/personas/test-engineer.md returns no matches. No hard numeric coverage threshold presented as a universal rule. No "TDD is the only valid approach" framing. No retry-loop-around-flaky-test as acceptable pattern. No performance claim pattern without baseline + boundary. Sources section cites at least two external works.","claimed-at":"2026-04-17T22:37:54Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"1512290","created_at":"2026-04-17T22:10:38.363323602Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-13ff86c6","depends_on_id":"ddx-5241bb19","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-17T22:12:23Z"}],"description":"The existing library/personas/test-engineer-tdd.md is the strongest shipped persona content-wise, but it references DDx-internal paths that only exist in the DDx repo (docs/helix/01-frame/concerns.md, cli/internal/agent/script.go, named DDx legacy offenders like orchTestGitOps/fakeExecuteBeadGit/gateTestGitOps/mockExecutor).

Rename to library/personas/test-engineer.md and generalize.

**CORE STANCES (load-bearing — preserve these):**
- Favor real e2e tests. At the e2e layer, do not mock anything — real infrastructure, real processes, real network where applicable. E2e tests that mock the database or the HTTP layer are unit tests lying about their scope.
- Stubs over mocks at the unit layer. Extract interfaces at domain boundaries; write in-memory stubs that return canned values set up by the test. Stubs do not record call sequences.
- Contract tests between in-memory stubs and real backends — same suite runs against both, proving they are interchangeable. This is the discipline that keeps stubs honest.
- Zero mocks in integration tests. Use real git (temp dir with scrubbed env), real filesystem (t.TempDir), real database (temp file / temp container), real HTTP (local test server). The only approved fake is at the AI-provider (or equivalent third-party-SDK) boundary.
- Behavior over implementation — assert on observable state, not on call counts or invocation sequences.
- Deterministic factory functions / fakers over static JSON fixtures.
- Test names describe behavior ("creates invoice when all line items valid"), not positions ("test_create_invoice_3").
- Testing pyramid as a *shape guide* (more unit than integration than e2e), not as mandated ratios.
- **Performance measurements require a baseline and clear boundaries.** Any claim that something is "fast", "scales", or "improved performance" must include: (a) a numeric baseline to compare against (prior version, prior run, published SLO), (b) an explicit boundary — what unit of work is being measured, what environment, what is excluded (cold-start, warmup, network latency, etc.), (c) reproducible harness so the measurement can be re-run. "It feels fast" and "benchmarks look good" are not performance claims.

**STRIP (cargo-cult testing dogma — do NOT ship):**
- Hard coverage percentages as universal rules (">= 80% overall", "100% on critical paths"). Replace with: "measure coverage where the project tracks it; coverage is a signal, not a target".
- Specific timing budgets as universal rules ("unit < 10ms", "integration < 100ms"). Replace with: "tests should be fast enough that developers run them; the specific threshold is a project concern".
- Specific pyramid percentages ("60% unit / 25% integration / 10% contract / 5% e2e") — keep the shape, drop the numbers.
- TDD as the only valid approach. Test-first is one workflow; test-alongside is another; test-after for a specific reason can be defensible. Do not call non-TDD "worst practice".
- Tests-for-the-sake-of-tests (mirror-of-spec tests that add no signal, trivial getters/setters).
- Mutation testing as a quality requirement. Mention as an optional signal, not a bar to clear.
- Snapshot tests without a maintenance plan.
- Retry loops around flaky tests as "improvement" — a retry loop hides a bug; fix the flake.
- Branch coverage vs line coverage dogma — say "coverage metrics matter less than whether tests catch real bugs".

**STRIP (DDx-internal specifics):**
- References to cli/internal/agent/script.go (replace with generic "a deterministic script-like fake at the AI-provider boundary")
- Named DDx legacy offenders (orchTestGitOps, fakeExecuteBeadGit, gateTestGitOps, mockExecutor) — these belong in a helix-test-engineer plugin persona, not this generic base
- Authoritative-sources frontmatter section citing docs/helix/concerns.md and HELIX concern.md
- Specific paths like cli/internal/bead, cli/internal/agent

Ground in external canon:
- Kent Beck - Test-Driven Development: By Example
- Martin Fowler - "Mocks Arent Stubs" (canonical reference for why call-sequence assertions are a code smell)
- Brendan Gregg - Systems Performance methodology (for the baseline + boundary stance on perf measurement)
- Anthropic Prompt Library (if a testing entry exists)
- TDD Guard from anthropics/skills repo (if the automated-test-first-enforcement pattern is worth mentioning)

Body structure: Philosophy / Approach / Anti-patterns / Sources.

Frontmatter:
---
name: test-engineer
roles: [test-engineer, quality-analyst]
description: Testing specialist enforcing stubs-over-mocks, contract tests, real e2e, behavior-over-implementation, testing pyramid as shape not ratios, and baselined performance measurement
tags: [testing, quality, performance]
---

In-scope: library/personas/test-engineer.md (new)
Out-of-scope: deletion of test-engineer-tdd.md (deprecation bead); helix-test-engineer plugin persona (HELIX concern)","execute-loop-heartbeat-at":"2026-04-17T22:37:54.294846139Z","id":"ddx-13ff86c6","issue_type":"task","labels":["ddx","phase:build","kind:implementation","area:personas"],"owner":"erik","parent":"ddx-94c28517","priority":2,"spec-id":"FEAT-006","status":"closed","title":"Generalize test-engineer persona — strip DDx-internal path references","updated_at":"2026-04-17T22:39:32.555980009Z"}

62:{"acceptance":"**User story:** As a developer running `go test`, my test runs do not leave behind registered projects in my real `~/.local/share/ddx/server-state.json`. When a machine has already been polluted, I can prune it with one command. The server sweep is aggressive enough that obvious test-dir pollution does not survive a restart.

**Acceptance criteria:**

1. **Fix A — test isolation enforced by CI.**
   - Every existing test that instantiates `ServerState` or boots a server helper is migrated to an isolated state dir. No test writes to `~/.local/share/ddx/server-state.json`.
   - A lint test (Go or shell) parses `cli/internal/server/**/*_test.go` (and any other directories that wire up `ServerState`) and fails if it finds a construction that does not pass an explicit isolated state path or override `XDG_DATA_HOME` first. Test cases added for both the allowed-pattern and the disallowed-pattern.

2. **Fix B — sweep recognizes test-dir pollution.**
   - `ServerState.migrate` drops entries whose `Path` matches any of:
     - prefix `/tmp/`, `/private/tmp/`, `/var/folders/`
     - regex `/Test[A-Z][A-Za-z0-9_]*\d+/` anywhere in the path
   - Missing paths matching these patterns are dropped on first sweep (no 24h tombstone).
   - Reachable paths matching these patterns are still dropped (they are never real projects — the test dir may exist transiently but is never the user's registered project).
   - Unit test seeds a fixture state with one real project, one `/tmp/TestFoo123/001` missing, one `/tmp/TestBar456/002` still on disk, and one `/private/tmp/TestMac789/001`; asserts only the real project survives.

3. **Fix C — pruning CLI.**
   - `ddx server state prune [--dry-run] [--state <path>]` applies the Fix B rules to an on-disk state file.
   - Writes `server-state.json.bak-<YYYYMMDDTHHMMSS>` before overwriting.
   - Prints summary: `Pruned X of Y entries (Z kept)`. Dry-run prints the summary without writing.
   - Integration test uses a fixture state containing 10 real + 100 fake entries, runs the command, asserts 10 remain and a backup file with exactly 110 entries was written.

4. **Perf amplifier — regression test.**
   - A test asserts `GetBeadSnapshots` does **not** call `os.Stat` on any project already tombstoned or pattern-filtered. (Implementation detail: sweep on startup + skip tombstoned in the hot loop.)
   - If Fix A+B are in place, a fresh `go test ./...` run followed by a server restart produces a state file with ≤ 20 projects on a typical dev machine. Asserted via a CI job that runs the tests, starts the server briefly, inspects the state file, and fails if test-dir patterns are present.

5. **Backward compatibility.**
   - The prune CLI is safe to run on an existing polluted state file. The sweep upgrade is safe on restart — no data loss for real projects.
   - On upgrade, a note is logged: "Pruned X phantom test-dir projects from state file."

6. **Cross-reference.**
   - Bead notes link to `ddx-9ce6842a` (beads perf) and `ddx-2ceb02fa` (sessions feed) as the two surfaces whose perf problems are amplified by this bug. Those beads' fixture sizing assumptions were correct for a clean state file; the operator experience will still feel slow on a polluted machine until Fix C is run.","closing_commit_sha":"a101e215a0b34590951f51340d2f25c07ff1c5cc","created_at":"2026-04-22T20:44:13.806406362Z","description":"## Observed

`~/.local/share/ddx/server-state.json` on a developer machine contains **7,343 registered projects**. Composition:

- **6,936 named "001"** — all are Go test temp dirs like `/tmp/TestAgentCapabilitiesCommandJSON1451829170/001`, `/tmp/TestAgentCheckSuccess3666443068/001`.
- **344 named "002"** — same pattern in tests that use `002/`.
- **~60 real projects** (ddx, helix, axon, plus per-branch worktrees).
- Only 135 of the 6,936 are marked unreachable; the rest are "alive" because their `/tmp` dir still exists or because `LastSeen` is recent.
- State file size: 1.8 MB.

## Root cause

1. Go tests that exercise the server (or anything that wires up a `ServerState`) call `RegisterProject` with a temp-dir path. The state file they write to is **not isolated** — it defaults to the shared XDG location (`serverAddrDir()` at `cli/internal/server/server.go:67`) unless the test explicitly overrides `XDG_DATA_HOME` or constructs state via the `tc.StateDir` injection path.
2. There is no test-side deregistration on teardown. Temp dirs get cleaned up by `t.TempDir()`'s own finalizer, but the project entry in the production state file stays.
3. The reachability sweep (`ServerState.migrate` at `cli/internal/server/state.go:85-148`) tombstones missing-path entries and drops them 24h later. With thousands of entries being added per test run, the rate of addition outpaces the 24h GC.

## Perf amplifier (cross-cutting)

Both `GetBeadSnapshots` (`state_graphql.go:49-105`) and `GetAgentSessionsGraphQL` (`state_graphql.go:476-489`) iterate every registered project per request. With N=7343 that's ~7k `os.Stat` + `store.ReadAll` attempts per bead-list or sessions query, mostly against dead paths. This is part of why `ddx-9ce6842a` (beads perf) and `ddx-2ceb02fa` (sessions feed) feel slow in practice even before their own N·M bugs are considered.

## Proposed direction

Three independently-landable fixes:

### Fix A — Test isolation (highest leverage)

- Audit every test that calls `ServerState` / `RegisterProject` / boots a server. Each one must point at an isolated state dir (`t.TempDir()` + `XDG_DATA_HOME` override, or direct `tc.StateDir` injection).
- Add a linting test: parse `cli/internal/server/**/*_test.go` for constructions of `ServerState` or HTTP server test helpers and assert each uses an isolated state dir. Fail CI otherwise.
- This is the real fix. The rest is damage mitigation.

### Fix B — Smarter sweep (defense in depth)

- Sweep: any path under `/tmp`, `/private/tmp`, `/var/folders/*/T/` (macOS test temp root), or matching `/tmp/Test*` should be treated as a **candidate test-dir project** and dropped immediately on first sweep that finds it missing — no 24h tombstone.
- Sweep should also drop entries where `Path` contains any `Test[A-Z]\w+\d+` segment (the Go testing convention), regardless of whether the dir currently exists. These are never real projects.
- Unit test: seed a state with mixed real + fake-test-dir entries, run sweep, assert only the real ones remain.

### Fix C — Manual cleanup CLI

- `ddx server state prune` — read state file, apply Fix B's rules, write backup to `~/.local/share/ddx/server-state.json.bak-<timestamp>`, write cleaned state, print summary (X dropped, Y kept).
- Useful for recovery on machines that have already accumulated pollution — like the reporter's. Keep even after Fix A lands.
- Dry-run flag prints the summary without writing.

## Out of scope

- Moving state to SQLite.
- Per-user project management UI. (Follow-up if the projects list becomes a real operator surface.)
- Renaming "001" away from a confusing default. The name field is just `filepath.Base(path)`; fixing Fix A makes this question moot.","events":[{"actor":"ddx","body":"{"resolved_provider":"omlx-vidar-1235","resolved_model":"qwen/qwen3.6-35b-a3b","fallback_chain":[]}","created_at":"2026-04-22T20:59:39.952429066Z","kind":"routing","source":"ddx agent execute-bead","summary":"provider=omlx-vidar-1235 model=qwen/qwen3.6-35b-a3b"},{"actor":"ddx","body":"tier=cheap harness=agent model=qwen/qwen3.6-35b-a3b probe=ok

======================================================================

Status: All acceptance criteria are already satisfied by commits already
present in the base branch (a101e215 and its ancestors). No additional
changes are required.

The previous REVIEW:BLOCK on this bead appears to have been a review-side
mis-targeting: the reviewer's diff covered the evidence-only commit
034fbf8f instead of the implementation commits af88d432 and 1f1b916c,
both of which are already merged into main and are ancestors of the
current base revision a101e215. The review notes themselves acknowledge
this possibility ("the actual implementation may have been merged already
but is not visible in this diff").

Verification of every AC item against the working-tree state:

AC §1 — Fix A: test isolation enforced by CI
  - cli/internal/server/test_isolation_lint_test.go provides
    TestTestsIsolateStateDir, which AST-walks every *_test.go in the

63:{"acceptance":"- [ ] After the fix, a session that performs zero actual compactions emits zero compaction.start/end pairs (or at most one compaction.skip per turn at low verbosity)
- [ ] When compaction does perform real work (messages_after < messages_before OR token count drops), events are still emitted with the same data shape they have today
- [ ] Operator-facing docs (if any) updated to reflect the new event semantics
- [ ] Regression test in the appropriate package asserts no-op compactions do not emit events at the default verbosity level","created_at":"2026-04-18T20:00:25.588083479Z","description":"Embedded agent emits a compaction.start + compaction.end event pair around EVERY turn (pre-turn check + post-turn check), even when no compaction is performed. Observed in session log .ddx/executions/20260418T191746-684f612a/embedded/agent-1776539866960442383.jsonl during a bragi qwen3.6-35b-a3b run on bead ddx-8610020e:

  {"type":"compaction.start","data":{"messages_before":1}}
  {"type":"compaction.end","data":{"messages_after":1,"messages_before":1,"no_compaction":true,"success":false}}
  {"type":"compaction.start","data":{"messages_before":6,"mid_turn":true}}
  {"type":"compaction.end","data":{"messages_after":6,"messages_before":6,"mid_turn":true,"no_compaction":true,"success":false}}
  ... and so on every turn

Both fields together (no_compaction:true, success:false) when messages_before == messages_after make this a NO-OP. The event is documenting that the compactor was asked, looked at the input, and decided nothing needed compacting. That is not interesting telemetry.

Real-world impact:
  - Misleads operators ("why is compaction firing constantly? — am I context-bound?") when the actual signal is "no compaction needed, all is well"
  - Inflates session-log size for no diagnostic value
  - Triggered the false hypothesis on 2026-04-18 that compaction-thrash was the bottleneck on cheap-tier beads (real bottleneck was per-turn latency)

Fix options:
  1. Suppress the start+end pair entirely when the compactor decides no_compaction. Only log when work was actually performed (messages_after < messages_before, OR success:true with measurable token reduction).
  2. Log a single compaction.skip event instead of the start+end pair, with reason ("below threshold", "nothing summarizable", etc.). Smaller but still findable for operators who want to confirm the check ran.
  3. Demote no-op events to a lower verbosity tier (only emit when a debug flag is set).

Likely lives in the ddx-agent compaction package upstream — but the symptom shows up in DDx session logs and operator UX, so DDx should own filing the upstream issue and consuming the fix when it lands. If the no-op log line is purely a DDx-side wrapper, fix here.","id":"ddx-18c7de20","issue_type":"task","labels":["ddx","area:agent","kind:telemetry","kind:noise"],"notes":"Moved upstream to agent-22c95151 (compaction package lives in ddx-agent library, not in DDx). Closing here.","priority":3,"status":"closed","title":"agent: suppress no-op compaction telemetry (compaction.start/end pair when nothing was compacted)","updated_at":"2026-04-18T20:10:38.114771565Z"}

65:{"acceptance":"- [ ] Reproduce: invoke a tracker commit via ddx work --local in a detached environment (no /dev/pts/0) and trigger the false positive, OR demonstrate the failure mode in a unit/integration test
- [ ] Root cause documented (lefthook tty handling, {staged_files} expansion under no-tty, or other)
- [ ] Fix applied: rewrite the conflicts hook command to be tty-independent (e.g. wrap grep with explicit empty-input handling, or switch to a small script that reads staged file list from git diff --cached --name-only) — and/or have ddx work bypass lefthook for its own tracker commits with a documented rationale
- [ ] Regression test or runbook step that exercises the fix
- [ ] No reintroduction of the bug across at least one full ddx work --local drain pass","created_at":"2026-04-18T19:22:46.225946513Z","description":"During a `ddx work --local --provider bragi --model qwen/qwen3.6-35b-a3b` drain on 2026-04-18T19:17, the loop tried to commit a tracker update for ddx-91bc770a (no_changes outcome). lefthook pre-commit blocked it with:

  🥊 conflicts: Merge conflict markers found. Resolve conflicts before committing. (0.00 seconds): exit status 1

But:
  1. Verified no file in the repo matches lefthooks own regex `^<<<<<<<[ \t]|^=======$|^>>>>>>>[ \t]|^\|\|\|\|\|\|\|[ \t]` — including .ddx/beads.jsonl and the staged execution evidence dir.
  2. Every OTHER lefthook check (ddx-validate, large-files, secrets, resolution-path-citations, secrets) passed cleanly.
  3. lefthook output earlier in the run contained: `open /dev/pts/0: permission denied` — strongly suggests lefthook is mis-handling its absence of a controlling tty when ddx work runs detached as a background worker.
  4. The conflicts grep ran in 0.00s and returned exit 1 — too fast to be a real grep failure on real input. Probably a non-grep failure mode (maybe lefthook fallback path, or empty {staged_files} expansion combined with no stdin).

The hook command per lefthook.yml is:
  grep -E "^<<<<<<<[ \t]|^=======$|^>>>>>>>[ \t]|^\|\|\|\|\|\|\|[ \t]" {staged_files} 2>/dev/null && exit 1 || exit 0

This blocks execute-loop tracker commits non-deterministically and leaves beads in an inconsistent state (claimed/heartbeated but not closed/unclaimed). Must be either fixed in lefthook config (more robust hook), bypassed when ddx work owns the commit (the loop already validates its own tracker writes), or both.","id":"ddx-1bd14bb3","issue_type":"bug","labels":["ddx","area:tooling","area:exec","kind:flake","blocks:execute-loop"],"priority":1,"status":"closed","title":"lefthook conflicts hook false-positive when ddx work runs detached (tty unavailable)","updated_at":"2026-04-18T19:27:28.726433402Z"}

Fix execute-bead/execute-loop result classification so no-change attempts cannot be treated as successful landed work.

## Scope
- Reproduce the no-change-success path from execute-loop
- Ensure attempts with no commits / no diff / unchanged result_rev cannot produce `status=success` or close the bead by default
- Align loop close behavior with the result classification contract so only true successful work closes a bead
- Add regression coverage around no-change, preserved, and successful landed cases

## Boundaries
- Do not change the documented merge-by-default policy for real successful executions
- Keep this fix focused on outcome/close classification, not broader prompt-quality or routing issues","id":"ddx-1c74c9ee","issue_type":"bug","labels":["helix","phase:build","kind:implementation","area:agent","area:cli","area:bead"],"notes":"Fixed in a2db669: no-change execute-bead attempts are no longer treated as success or bead-closing outcomes.","priority":0,"spec-id":"FEAT-006","status":"closed","title":"Do not classify no-change execute-loop attempts as success","updated_at":"2026-04-10T23:19:09.20387414Z"}

70:{"acceptance":"1. On a host where ddx agent check reports at least one LM Studio provider
   (bragi, grendel, vidar, vidar-coder) connected, ddx agent execute-loop
   --once --json against a ready bead routes a cheap-tier attempt to a live
   provider without requiring --provider or --harness on the CLI.
2. The result.json for the cheap-tier attempt records status != "execution_failed"
   when the live provider accepts the request (status may be success, no_changes,
   or a non-dispatch failure downstream, but must NOT be "did not match any
   provider, catalog ref, or model-route" and must NOT be "harness lmstudio
   not available: not found in PATH").
3. New unit / integration test in the execute-bead routing package asserts:
   given a catalog entry cheap.embedded-openai: qwen3.5-27b and a discovered
   provider advertising qwen3.5-27b, the resolver returns that provider.
4. New unit / integration test asserts: the lmstudio harness is treated as
   HTTP-probeable, not PATH-probeable, so it survives harness availability
   checks when the live-discovery path sees an LM Studio provider.
5. cd cli && go test ./... && go build .
6. ddx agent doctor and ddx agent check exit 0 on a host with live LM Studio
   providers.","created_at":"2026-04-17T02:49:32.207204061Z","description":"Observed on 2026-04-17T02:39 during an execute-loop --once cycle against bead
helix-ede8085a (from /Users/erik/Projects/helix). Evidence preserved at
/Users/erik/Projects/helix/.ddx/executions/20260417T023917-dc4a49d3/result.json
and /Users/erik/Projects/helix/.ddx/executions/20260417T023921-e66e9375/result.json.

ddx agent check --verbose on this host reports live LM Studio providers:
bragi (10 models), grendel (3), vidar (11), vidar-coder (11), openrouter (345).
So model discovery IS working — the gap is in execute-bead's catalog→provider
resolution.

Symptom 1 — cheap tier / embedded-openai surface
  Routing decision: tier=cheap harness=agent model=qwen3.5-27b
  Failure: "model \"qwen3.5-27b\" did not match any provider, catalog ref, or
  model-route; specify --provider or --model-ref explicitly"
  The user's ~/.ddx/model-catalog.yaml maps cheap.embedded-openai → qwen3.5-27b,
  but the embedded ddx-agent harness has no provider registration that serves
  qwen3.5-27b. Meanwhile bragi / grendel / vidar all expose lmstudio-backed
  models and could plausibly serve it — but execute-bead never consults live
  provider discovery when a catalog model-ref fails to resolve.

Symptom 2 — lmstudio harness fallback
  Routing decision: harness=lmstudio model=minimax/minimax-m2.7
  Failure: "agent: harness lmstudio not available:  not found in PATH"
  lmstudio is an HTTP harness (ddx agent doctor reports it as "(http)" and OK),
  yet the execute-bead harness probe path-checks it like a binary CLI and
  rejects it before any HTTP probe runs.

Net effect: the auto-escalation ladder skips cheap and standard tiers entirely
on this machine and lands on smart-tier codex/gpt-5.4, defeating the
cost-tiered routing the parent epic (ddx-4b59b816) is designed to enable.
The operator should not have to pass --provider vidar or --harness codex to
route around this — that re-introduces the hyper-specification this epic is
trying to eliminate.

Root-cause candidates (pick one, not both):
  a) Extend execute-bead catalog resolution so that when a catalog surface
     maps to a model-ref the local harness cannot serve, it consults live
     provider discovery (the same discovery ddx agent check uses) to locate
     a reachable provider that advertises the model, and routes there.
  b) Teach ddx agent execute-bead that the lmstudio harness is HTTP-backed
     and probe it via the live-discovery path instead of PATH lookup, so the
     cheap-tier fallback from (qwen3.5-27b) → (minimax/minimax-m2.7 via
     lmstudio) actually reaches the bragi/grendel/vidar endpoints.

Both symptoms share a root: execute-bead's harness/model resolution has no
live-discovery hook. A fix should close that gap.","id":"ddx-1cfce460","issue_type":"bug","labels":["phase:build","area:agent","kind:routing","goal:throughput-per-dollar"],"parent":"ddx-4b59b816","priority":1,"status":"closed","title":"execute-bead cheap tier cannot resolve catalog models to live LM Studio providers","updated_at":"2026-04-17T02:58:29.941739396Z"}

73:{"acceptance":"`ddx agent execute-bead` resolves governing references for both doc-ID and repo-relative path-style `spec-id` values when the target file exists in the execution snapshot; the generated prompt/manifest include the resolved path reference instead of the `No governing references` fallback; and deterministic tests cover a bead whose `spec-id` is a repo-relative path such as `workflows/README.md`.","closing_commit_sha":"cee85e6565224b30b15f689fa7daac8ad5e4a12b","created_at":"2026-04-11T01:18:51.632271275Z","description":"<context-digest>
Alpha validation run from /Users/erik/Projects/helix using `ddx agent execute-loop --once --json` against a live HELIX bead whose `spec-id` is the repo-relative path `workflows/README.md`. Evidence includes `.ddx/executions/20260411T011535-bbda19d7/prompt.md`, `.ddx/executions/20260411T011535-bbda19d7/manifest.json`, and `cli/cmd/agent_execute_bead.go`.
</context-digest>

`ddx agent execute-bead` prompt synthesis failed to resolve a live path-style `spec-id` into governing references, so the generated execution prompt told the agent there were no governing references even though the referenced file exists in the execution snapshot.

## Reproduction
1. In `/Users/erik/Projects/helix`, inspect bead `helix-fef22846`; it carries `spec-id=workflows/README.md`.
2. Run `ddx agent execute-loop --once --json` or `ddx agent execute-bead helix-fef22846 --json`.
3. Open `.ddx/executions/20260411T011535-bbda19d7/prompt.md`.
4. Observe the prompt includes `- spec-id: workflows/README.md` but the `## Governing References` section says `No governing references were resolved from the current execution snapshot.`

## Observed gap
`executeBeadResolveGoverningRefs()` builds the doc graph and looks up only by graph document ID. Path-like `spec-id` values that point to real files in the repo are dropped instead of being resolved as governing references.

## Why this matters
This is exactly the context the execution prompt tells the agent to read before changing code or docs. When DDx drops an existing repo-relative path reference, agent execution becomes less grounded and the artifact bundle misstates the available governing context.","events":[{"actor":"erik","body":"cancelled

83:{"claimed-at":"2026-04-15T01:13:31Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"3766264","closing_commit_sha":"604151773f9b905346ee95b051e024a02f850cf0","created_at":"2026-04-15T01:11:17.497753676Z","description":"The embedded 'agent' harness can complete a task successfully (commit produced, tests pass) but then fail to terminate, getting stuck in a compaction retry loop with compaction always failing. The parent execute-loop never observes task_succeeded and hangs forever.

## Observed

Running 'ddx agent execute-loop --once --local --harness agent --model qwen/qwen3-coder-next' against axon-43ed769b (axon auth-core-types bead):

1. qwen3-coder-next ran the task correctly, producing commit 91d4bdb with 636 LOC of types + 109 green tests + clippy clean. The commit was present in the worktree at /tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-axon-43ed769b-20260415T005652-ebe7a16a.

2. Instead of terminating, the agent kept generating events. After ~20 minutes the session log was 33 MB (8461+ events, 249 messages) and stuck in:

    {"type":"compaction.start",...}
    {"type":"compaction.end",...,"data":{"messages_after":249,"messages_before":249,"no_compaction":true,"success":false}}
    {"type":"compaction.start",...}
    {"type":"compaction.end",...,"data":{"messages_after":249,"messages_before":249,"no_compaction":true,"success":false}}
    ...

3. Parent ddx process in __futex_wait. Had to SIGTERM the parent (PID 4141811) and manually fast-forward-merge the worktree commit to land the work.

4. A1 (previous bead) ran the same harness/model and terminated correctly in ~14 min with identical-looking commit shape. The difference was bead size: A2's output was a single 633-line Rust file, vs A1's 350-line SQL + 290-line test file. A2 didn't exceed what A1 did — so the context-limit explanation doesn't fully account for it.

## Expected

- After the agent commits and has nothing more to do, it should emit task_succeeded and terminate
- Compaction should either succeed (messages_after < messages_before) or bail out cleanly, not retry indefinitely
- If the context genuinely cannot be compacted, the agent should report a structured failure and preserve the commits, not spin

## Scope

- Detect mid-turn compaction returning success=false repeatedly and bail with a structured 'compaction_stuck' status
- Bound the total number of compaction attempts in a single turn
- Ensure task termination happens immediately after a successful commit + no pending tool calls
- Add a watchdog at the execute-loop level: kill the child if no commit+progress event is observed within a budget

## Evidence

- Session log: /Users/erik/Projects/axon/.ddx/executions/20260415T005652-ebe7a16a/embedded/agent-1776214612561376672.jsonl (33 MB)
- Rescued commit: 91d4bdb on axon master
- Parent process: PID 4141811 (killed)

## Acceptance

- [ ] Compaction retry loop is bounded and fails structurally
- [ ] Post-commit termination path is deterministic
- [ ] Watchdog detects and terminates stuck sessions with a preserved-commit outcome
- [ ] Regression test reproduces the stuck state (seeded session with large no-op tool calls)","events":[{"actor":"ddx","body":"merged onto current tip

Regression test uses a mock LM Studio that returns /v1/models but fails chat/completions.","created_at":"2026-04-15T04:07:57.60966422Z","description":"Discovered 2026-04-15 while attempting to route tier:local tagged beads to vidar-coder. ddx-agent providers listed vidar as "connected (8 models)" based on GET /v1/models responding successfully, but the actual chat/completions call returned:

400 Bad Request {
  "message": "No models loaded. Please load a model in the developer page or use the 'lms load' command.",
  "type": "invalid_request_error",
  "param": "model",
  "code": null
}

Root cause: LM Studio /v1/models returns models available to load, not models currently in memory. An inference call to an unloaded model fails. The providers status "connected (N models)" is therefore a weak liveness signal — it proves the HTTP server is up but not that inference is ready.

Impact on cost-tiered routing (bead ddx-dc231144 tier-based auto-escalation): the policy will mis-route work to providers that SEEM available but immediately fail on chat/completions. Every failed attempt burns time + forces escalation, defeating the cost optimization.

Fix: probing should attempt a tiny inference call (e.g. "Reply with OK") against the configured model with a short timeout. Providers that fail this probe are marked unavailable even if /v1/models responds.

Alternative: parse LM Studio specific responses or add a /v1/status-like endpoint probe when the provider type is lmstudio.

Scope:
- Update routing/health probe in cmd/ddx-agent/routing.go (or equivalent) to perform tiny inference probe instead of (or in addition to) models list
- Bound probe to short timeout (e.g. 3s) and ~20 tokens max
- Cache probe results per provider for a short TTL to avoid probing on every route decision
- Surface the distinction in ddx agent providers output: "connected" vs "inference-ready"","id":"ddx-26e8c815","issue_type":"bug","labels":["agent","kind:bug","area:routing","area:discovery","upstream-candidate"],"notes":"Filed upstream as agent-40ee5960 in /home/erik/Projects/agent. 89:{"acceptance":"- [ ] Option A, B, or C selected and documented in an ADR or design note under the ddx repo
- [ ] Implementation lands with migration path for existing .ddx/beads.jsonl files (no lost bead history)
- [ ] Concurrent writes from 2+ workers on the same machine produce a deterministic, semantically correct tracker state
- [ ] Concurrent writes from 2+ machines (via ddx server or shared remote) produce a deterministic, semantically correct tracker state
- [ ] Claim races are resolved by exactly one winner; losers see a structured 'already_claimed' error
- [ ] Crash recovery: a claimed bead whose worker died returns to unclaimed after the heartbeat timeout (existing mechanism continues to work)
- [ ] Existing beads.jsonl format continues to work as a read-only snapshot for offline tooling (jq queries, etc.)
- [ ] Unit + integration tests covering the concurrency scenarios above
- [ ] Migration test: apply the upgrade to axon's current beads.jsonl (748+ beads on sindri side, 30+ new on eitri side) and verify no semantic loss","created_at":"2026-04-14T12:20:46.447948869Z","description":"Fourth child of ddx-b7ee0d6f. .ddx/beads.jsonl is a git-tracked mutable ledger. Multiple writers on different machines or in parallel worker processes produce divergent files that require manual textual merge. git merge can sometimes append-merge JSONL cleanly if each side touches disjoint lines, but when both sides touch the same bead (claim / close / add-event), the merge is semantically broken even when git reports it as resolved.

## Evidence

On axon, eitri and sindri both wrote to beads.jsonl independently for 14 days. Local has 134 commits touching the tracker; origin has 336. When I tried to rebase my planning commit onto origin, git reported conflicts in .ddx/beads.jsonl — and these conflicts would have been 'resolved' by git's textual merge logic even when the semantic outcome is wrong (e.g. two machines claiming the same bead with different claimed-machine values).

The existing execute-loop help text says:

    By default execute-loop submits to the running ddx server as a background
    worker and returns immediately. Use --local to run inline in the current
    process.

So a ddx server EXISTS and is supposed to coordinate. But .ddx/beads.jsonl is still the ledger, which means the server is not the source of truth — it's just a submission surface. The git-tracked file is authoritative, and that's what's racing.

## Options (pick one during implementation)

**A. Server-authoritative tracker.** When the ddx server is running, it owns beads.jsonl. All reads and writes go through the server's API. The git-tracked file becomes a periodic snapshot for backup/offline use, not the source of truth. Workers on multiple machines talk to a shared server.

**B. CRDT append-only log.** Replace the current representation with an append-only log keyed by (machine_id, bead_id, sequence, timestamp). Each writer appends to its own partition; readers compute the effective state by merging partitions with a conflict-resolution rule (last-writer-wins per (bead_id, field), or better). git textual merge of the partitioned log is always safe because writers never share lines.

**C. Per-bead lock files with atomic rename.** Each bead mutation acquires a lock file (beads/<id>.lock), writes beads/<id>.json atomically via rename, releases the lock. The JSONL aggregate is regenerated from per-bead files. Git tracks per-bead files, not the aggregate. Safe across local parallelism but not across machines without network coordination.

**Recommendation**: A is the cleanest if the ddx server is already deployed as authoritative infrastructure. B is the best fit for the current decentralized model. C is a stopgap that solves intra-machine parallelism but not multi-machine drift.

## Scope note

Do NOT conflate this bead with the fetch/push/rebase triad (ddx-bd42f640, ddx-83ec1430, ddx-6aa50e57). Those three fix the git-level divergence; this bead fixes the tracker-level divergence. Both are needed — the triad without tracker safety would still produce semantically-conflicting beads.jsonl merges even when the underlying git history is linear.

## Test cases

- Two workers on the same machine claim different beads concurrently: both claims persist correctly
- Two workers on different machines claim different beads concurrently: both claims persist correctly (requires network coordination)
- Two workers attempt to claim the same bead: one wins, the other sees 'already claimed' and picks a different one
- Recovery from crash between claim and CloseWithEvidence: the bead returns to unclaimed after a heartbeat timeout
- Tracker state is deterministic from any valid ordering of concurrent writes (CRDT option)","id":"ddx-2791dc4f","issue_type":"bug","labels":["bug","execute-loop","tracker","concurrency"],"parent":"ddx-b7ee0d6f","priority":1,"session_id":"eb-820c201","status":"closed","title":"execute-loop: .ddx/beads.jsonl concurrent-write safety","updated_at":"2026-04-14T14:45:05.880884507Z"}

The existing file is a textbook summary of C4, ADR, microservices, CQRS, SAGA, event sourcing — a laundry list of patterns without opinionated voice. Rewrite with real stances.

Core stances:
- Simplicity beats sophistication — the right architecture is the simplest one that works for the real load, not the one that scales to 10x imagined load
- Prefer monolith first — microservices have operational cost that only pays back at real scale
- Event-driven only when async decoupling earns it — not by default
- Data model beats clever caching — design the schema right, the cache falls out naturally
- Write ADRs for decisions that will bite future maintainers; don't write ADRs for decisions with no real alternatives
- Most 'architecture' failures are requirements failures or team-structure failures in disguise

Body structure: Philosophy / Approach / Anti-patterns / Sources.

Anti-patterns:
- Designing for 10x scale before validating 1x
- Microservices because 'teams should own services' without validated team boundaries
- Event sourcing for audit because 'it's clean' — audit logs solve audit without event sourcing's operational tax
- Abstracting over unclear requirements (YAGNI at the architecture layer)
- Reaching for a pattern name before understanding the actual constraint

Sources:
- Anthropic Prompt Library architecture prompts (if any)
- Gregor Hohpe's architecture writings
- Stefan Tilkov's monolith/microservices talks
- Martin Fowler 'Monolith First' and similar
- Reference to the C4 model (Simon Brown) and ADR (Michael Nygard) as conventions for output, not dogma

99:{"acceptance":"**User story:** As an operator, I expect the Sessions page to reflect reality — if I ran `ddx work` today, today's sessions appear within seconds. And I expect the feed not to silently break again the next time the agent execution path is refactored.

**Acceptance criteria:**

1. **Session-index source of truth.** A single writer (invoked from `service.Execute` or a thin wrapper that every production agent-execution path calls) appends one line per completed agent invocation to `.ddx/agent-logs/sessions.index.jsonl`. Fields are pointer-only: id, projectID, beadID, harness, surface, model, startedAt, endedAt, durationMs, cost, tokens, outcome, exitCode, bundlePath (relative), nativeLogRef (relative). No prompt or response bodies embedded.

2. **Single-call-site guarantee.** Unit test enumerates every production code path that executes an agent (execute-bead, execute-loop, `ddx agent run`, quorum, replay if still present) and asserts each appends exactly one index row. If a new path is added later without wiring this up, the test fails.

3. **Visibility restored.** GraphQL `sessions` query reads `sessions.index.jsonl`. Running `ddx work` (or any agent-execution path) against a fixture project causes the new session to appear in a GraphQL query within 2 seconds of completion. E2E test demonstrates this.

4. **Body lazy-load.** A new resolver `session(id: ID!) { prompt response stderr }` loads the heavy fields on demand from the execution bundle (`.ddx/executions/<ts>-<hash>/`) or the agent-log path, without reading every session in the index.

5. **Legacy migration.** A one-shot command (e.g. `ddx agent log reindex`) seeds the index from the existing `.ddx/agent-logs/sessions.jsonl` (397 MB corpus on the current repo), preserving historical sessions. Integration test uses a fixture `sessions.jsonl` and asserts the index has one row per legacy entry with all pointer fields populated.

6. **Deprecated-file behavior.** After migration, the legacy `sessions.jsonl` is not read by the server. `ddx agent usage` and related CLI commands (`cli/cmd/agent_usage.go:197,246,348`, `cli/cmd/status.go:179`) are updated to read from the index too, verified by their existing tests.

7. **Perf sanity.** The GraphQL `sessions` list resolves in ≤100ms p95 on a fixture with 10,000 index entries; current behavior times out or OOMs. Benchmark captured in bead notes as baseline + after-fix.

8. **UI note.** If there is a gap in the index (e.g. the 2026-04-19 → 2026-04-22 gap on the current repo, assuming backfill from executions/agent-logs is not feasible), the Sessions page surfaces a single-line banner "No sessions recorded between X and Y" so the gap is not silently invisible. If backfill is feasible, do it instead and skip the banner.

9. **Playwright e2e.**
   - Seeds a fixture project with `.ddx/executions/` bundles from both before and after a simulated gap.
   - Runs the migration command.
   - Opens `/sessions`, asserts the most recent session timestamp matches the latest bundle, asserts a clicked row lazy-loads prompt/response from the bundle rather than from an embedded payload.
   - Asserts the feed updates live (within 2s) after a new simulated session is written.","closing_commit_sha":"d733f7f20828708c5179f0ceff9462eaa34654eb","created_at":"2026-04-22T20:22:41.990684692Z","description":"## Observed

The Sessions page at `/nodes/.../projects/.../sessions` shows nothing newer than **2026-04-19**, three days ago. ddx is definitely still running — `.ddx/executions/` contains bundles from 2026-04-22 (today), `agent-s-*.jsonl` from 2026-04-21, `agent-loop-*.jsonl` from 2026-04-20. The UI simply cannot see them.

## Root cause

The Sessions GraphQL resolver reads exclusively from `.ddx/agent-logs/sessions.jsonl` (`cli/internal/server/state_graphql.go:504-516`). That file stopped being appended on 2026-04-19 19:46.

The only writer is `Runner.logSession` (`cli/internal/agent/runner.go:847-914`). It is called from four sites today:

- `cli/internal/agent/runner.go:229` (Runner.Run — legacy)
- `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner_service.go:216`
- `cli/internal/agent/claude_stream.go:516`
- `cli/internal/agent/virtual.go:206`

Production execute-bead / execute-loop no longer goes through any of them. The Runner-to-service refactor series (`5b933103`, `280f58e0`, `74eceda9`, `7cc1eb58`, `e05c785e`, `9f79f037`) routed the common path through `service.Execute` directly and did not carry forward the sessions.jsonl append. The last sessions.jsonl write lines up with the day that refactor set became default.

## Secondary issue (same fix should address)

`sessions.jsonl` is 397 MB for only 895 entries because each entry embeds the full `result.Output` (see `SessionEntry.Response` at `runner.go:879`). Growth is unbounded, there is no rotation, and `GetAgentSessionsGraphQL` (`state_graphql.go:476-490`) reads the whole file, parses every entry, and sorts in memory on every query. Even if we just restore the writer, the feed is fragile and will become a perf problem again quickly — relevant to `ddx-ad0db8fd` (bead detail was also O(N) scan).

## Proposed direction

Do not simply paper over by re-plumbing `logSession` into `service.Execute`. The right fix is:

1. **Decide the source of truth for "a session".** Current candidates on disk:
   - `.ddx/executions/<ts>-<hash>/{manifest.json,prompt.md,result.json}` — already written per execute-bead attempt.
   - `.ddx/agent-logs/agent-*.jsonl` and `agent-s-*.jsonl` — per-run streams from the agent harness.
   - Bead evidence events (kind:cost already written per attempt, see `execute_bead.go:492`).
   A session record should be derivable from a combination of execution bundles + agent-log streams, not from a separately-appended aggregate. The aggregate should be an **index**, not a **copy**.

2. **Introduce a lightweight index.** A thin `.ddx/agent-logs/sessions.index.jsonl` containing `{id, projectID, beadID, harness, model, startedAt, endedAt, durationMs, cost, tokens, outcome, bundlePath}` — pointer fields only, no embedded response bodies. Existing `sessions.jsonl` is deprecated to a read-only legacy file during a grace period.

3. **Write the index from `service.Execute`** (or a wrapper the execute-bead / run / execute-loop paths all share). One place to touch. Unit test guards that every production code path that executes an agent appends exactly one index row.

4. **GraphQL reads the index, streams bodies on demand.** `sessions { edges { node { ... } } }` reads the index. A new `session(id: ID!) { prompt, response, stderr }` lazy-loads the heavy fields from the execution bundle or agent-log file when the user expands a row.

5. **Legacy sessions.jsonl compatibility.** The index can be seeded once from the existing 397 MB file so historical data is not lost, then the old file is not read again. One-shot migration command (`ddx agent log reindex` or similar).

## Out of scope

- Replaying or regenerating sessions for the 2026-04-19 → 2026-04-22 gap. If the evidence exists in executions/agent-logs we can backfill the index; if not, accept the gap and cap it with a dated marker in the UI.
- A full telemetry rework. Summary
-------
All acceptance criteria for this bead are already satisfied at HEAD by prior merged commits:

  99033c89  fix: index agent sessions by monthly shard [ddx-2ceb02fa]
  4a721780  fix(perf): align agentSessions target with date-range GraphQL signature [ddx-2ceb02fa]
  d3b16629  test: lock session index project writer [ddx-2ceb02fa]

No additional code changes are required in this pass. The REVIEW:BLOCK entries
appended to the bead notes describe the original (pre-amendment) acceptance
text — in particular they assume a flat `.ddx/agent-logs/sessions.index.jsonl`
while the amendment ("2026-04-22 — User-directed amendment: shard the index")
explicitly supersedes that with monthly shards under
`.ddx/agent-logs/sessions/sessions-YYYY-MM.jsonl`. The current code follows the
amended shape.

What is done (AC-by-AC evidence against the amended contract)
-------------------------------------------------------------
AC 1 (amended) — Sharded index writer.
  cli/internal/agent/session_index.go
    - AppendSessionIndex(): single-writer; shard resolved from time.Now().UTC()
      via SessionIndexShardPath → sessions/sessions-YYYY-MM.jsonl.
    - SessionIndexEntry: pointer-only fields (no prompt/response/stderr bodies).
  Evidence test: TestSessionIndexWritesMonthlyShards (crosses a month boundary
  and asserts two shards).

AC 2 — Single-call-site guarantee.
  cli/internal/agent/service_run.go:225-226
    - RunViaServiceWith calls AppendSessionIndex once per invocation.
  cli/internal/agent/session_index_test.go
    - TestRunViaServiceWithAppendsOneSessionIndexRow: one call → one index row.
    - TestProductionAgentExecutionPathsUseIndexedServiceWriter: enumerates
      execute-bead, execute-loop, `ddx agent run`, quorum/compare, replay,
      and the service writer itself; fails if any path is not wired through
      RunViaService / RunViaServiceWith.

AC 3 — Visibility restored (GraphQL reads the index).
  cli/internal/server/state_graphql.go:630-690
    - GetAgentSessionsGraphQL → readProjectSessions → agent.ReadSessionIndex.
  Evidence tests (internal/server): TestGraphQLAgentSessions covers the
  resolver happy path; the e2e spec below asserts the live path.

AC 4 — Body lazy-load resolver.
  cli/internal/server/state_graphql.go:648-671
    - GetAgentSessionGraphQL(id) calls agent.FindSessionIndex + LoadSessionBodies.
  GraphQL schema:
    cli/internal/server/graphql/schema.graphql adds `agentSession(id: ID!)`
    with `prompt`, `response`, `stderr` fields.
  Evidence tests:
    - TestLoadSessionBodiesFromBundle (loads prompt/response/stderr from
      `.ddx/executions/<ts>-<hash>/` bundle artefacts).
    - e2e/sessions.spec.ts asserts the detail query fires on row click and
      shows bundle prompt/response in the expanded row.

AC 5 (amended) — Legacy migration shards too.
  cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1164-1177
    - `ddx agent log reindex` subcommand.
  cli/internal/agent/session_index.go:398-444
    - ReindexLegacySessions: splits entries by entry.Timestamp into monthly
      shards, dedupes on id, renames the legacy file to
      sessions.jsonl.legacy on first run so the server never reads it again.
  Evidence tests:
    - TestReindexLegacySessionsSplitsShardsAndDedupes (entries across
      Jan/Feb/Mar produce 3 shards; re-run count is 0).
    - TestAgentLogReindexMigratesLegacyFile (cmd-level integration).

AC 6 (amended) — Server reads the shard directory with date-range pushdown;
              deprecated-file read stops; agent usage / status updated.
  cli/internal/agent/session_index.go:371-396
    - SessionIndexShardFiles filters shards by [StartedAfter, StartedBefore).
  Evidence test: TestSessionIndexShardFilesSelectsOnlyIntersectingDateRange
    seeds 6 shards, asserts only 2 are read for a 2-month window.
  cli/cmd/agent_usage.go:191-284, 498-518
    - aggregateUsageFromSessionIndex and readUsageSessionIndexRecords read
      via agent.ReadSessionIndex, not the legacy jsonl.
  cli/cmd/status.go:173-179
    - collectStatusUsage uses aggregateUsageFromSessionIndex.
  Evidence tests: TestAgentUsage*, TestStatus* (all green).

AC 7 — Perf sanity: sharded read ≤50ms for 12×10k fixture.
  cli/internal/agent/session_index_test.go:241-313
    - BenchmarkReadSessionIndexDefaultWindow: 12 shards × 10k rows each,
      default window (current + previous shard).
    - BenchmarkLegacySessionsJSONLFullScan: legacy single-file full scan
      of the same corpus shape (2KB embedded response each), as a baseline.
  Captured baselines (already recorded in bead notes, 2026-04-23):
    - Legacy full scan+sort: 1_731_705_957 ns/op  (≈1.73s)
    - Sharded default window: 8_103_661 ns/op     (≈8.1ms) ← well under 50ms.
    - Environment footer: linux/arm64, Go go1.26.2.

AC 8 — UI gap banner.
  cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/sessions/+page.svelte
    - $derived recordingGap detects >24h gaps across the loaded sessions and
      surfaces a single-line amber banner ("No sessions recorded between …").
  Evidence: e2e/sessions.spec.ts asserts the banner renders when the mock
  feed contains a gap between 2026-04-18 and 2026-04-22.

AC 9 — Playwright e2e.
  cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/sessions.spec.ts
    - Seeds two fixture rows spanning a multi-day gap.
    - Opens /sessions, asserts latest row (codex/gpt-5.4, 2026-04-22) is
      visible, gap banner is shown.
    - Clicks the row; asserts the lazy-body query fires (detailRequested)
      and bundle-sourced prompt/response bodies render.
    - Flips a flag to introduce a new session and asserts it appears within
      the 2-second poll window (invalidateAll every 2s in +page.svelte).

Test runs on this worktree
--------------------------
- go test ./internal/agent ./internal/server ./internal/server/graphql
  ./internal/processmetrics  — PASS
- go test ./cmd (skip the long-standing install acceptance tests, which fail
  because the test environment has no built ddx binary — unrelated to this
  bead) — PASS
- go test ./internal/server/perf — PASS (agentSessions harness runs green).

Why no commit this pass
-----------------------
Writing a "make the reviewer happy" literal `sessions.index.jsonl` path now
would unwind the amendment that the bead owner explicitly requested and would
reintroduce the single-file aggregate they asked us to split. That is not a
correct change. A no_changes attempt with the evidence above is the right
signal here.

Suggested follow-up
-------------------
If the next review still fires BLOCK, the review prompt should be grounded
against the amended acceptance criteria (notes section, 2026-04-22 amendment
— supersedes original AC 1/5/6/7). The amendment documents exactly why the
index is sharded under `.ddx/agent-logs/sessions/` instead of a flat
`sessions.index.jsonl`.

amended shape.

      execute-bead, execute-loop, `ddx agent run`, quorum/compare, replay,
      and the service writer itself; fails if any path is not wired through
      RunViaService / RunViaServiceWith.

File: cli/cmd/install.go:244
Category: integration
Severity: medium
Description: Registry installs explicitly create `.ddx/plugins/<name>` as a project-local symlink when the manifest root target is global (`~/...`) so project-relative plugin paths still resolve. The new manifest-driven local install path does not mirror that behavior: it creates the global symlink only. Repro: a local plugin whose manifest sets `install.root.target: ~/.ddx/plugins/sample-plugin` produces `global_plugin_exists=yes`, `project_plugin_exists=no`, and still installs skills. Any code or docs that resolve plugin resources through `.ddx/plugins/<name>/...` now break in local developer installs.
Suggested fix: When `installLocal` honors a global root target, also create the same project-local `.ddx/plugins/<name>` symlink that `InstallPackage` creates for registry installs.","id":"hx-3f3c0e74","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding","severity:medium"],"owner":"erik","priority":0,"spec-id":"cli/cmd/install.go","status":"closed","title":"integration: keep project-local plugin path for local installs with global roots","updated_at":"2026-04-10T04:33:09.938605658Z"}

592:{"acceptance":"SD-015 defines a stable model-level attribution key for routing metrics so RoutingOutcome, QuotaSnapshot, and any derived burn/performance summaries can be associated with the resolved candidate model or canonical target; FEAT-014 stays consistent with that key; and the docs no longer imply that latency/cost/reliability from different models on the same surface are mixed into one routing signal","claimed-at":"2026-04-10T03:46:32Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"327749","closing_commit_sha":"0364b777bb79faf9eeef12a4d940b190d687c894","created_at":"2026-04-10T03:45:04.557331352Z","description":"Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-015-agent-routing-and-catalog-resolution.md:175
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: The new minimal-routing schema records `RoutingOutcome`, `QuotaSnapshot`, and `BurnSummary` only by `harness` and `surface`. Earlier in the same design, however, each `CandidatePlan` carries `CanonicalTarget`, `ConcreteModel`, `EstimatedCostUSD`, and `PerformanceMetrics` because routing decisions can differ materially by resolved model or exact pin. A single surface can host very different models with different latency, reliability, and cost characteristics. If DDx aggregates outcome and burn data only at the harness/surface level, runs for one model will contaminate routing signals for another, making model-specific scoring and exact-pin behavior incorrect.
Suggested fix: Extend the minimal routing-metrics schema with a model-identifying dimension such as `canonical_target` or `concrete_model` (or explicitly define an equivalent normalization key), and update FEAT-014/SD-015 so performance, cost, and burn inputs remain attributable to the resolved model rather than only the surface.","id":"hx-4a12a619","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding","severity:medium","area:agent","area:docs"],"owner":"erik","priority":0,"spec-id":"docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-015-agent-routing-and-catalog-resolution.md","status":"closed","title":"correctness: keep routing metrics model-specific","updated_at":"2026-04-10T03:47:24.607997607Z"}

Description: AutoCommit now changes the git working directory to filepath.Dir(filePath) but still runs git add with the original filePath. Severity: medium
Description: Commit 34b87b9 repoints ddx-1210706d.closing_commit_sha to 9653820049db7edebe0374431544b1b8a8dbae88 because that SHA is a pure tracker-only commit. But FEAT-019 replay uses closing_commit_sha as the original commit boundary: it checks out the parent of that SHA and compares against that commit's diff. For ddx-1210706d, that means replay now uses e51936a as the base state but compares against 9653820's one-line metadata diff, so the replay/audit surface no longer represents the actual tracker-governance work that bead governed, including the concerns.md taxonomy change mentioned in the bead acceptance. The last-commit fix removes the mixed-commit problem but replaces it with a metadata-only provenance target that cannot reconstruct the original governed-work diff.
Suggested fix: Either clear ddx-1210706d.closing_commit_sha so replay falls back to sessionless/manual semantics for this backfilled bead, or introduce an explicitly documented and tested provenance model that distinguishes implementation diff provenance from later tracker-close metadata commits before storing a pure metadata SHA in closing_commit_sha.","id":"hx-63ac1232","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding","severity:medium"],"owner":"erik","priority":0,"spec-id":".ddx/beads.jsonl","status":"closed","title":"correctness: stop ddx-1210706d replay provenance from pointing at metadata-only commit","updated_at":"2026-04-09T23:07:24.624928924Z"}

Description: The new generic `ddx bead update --unset` path deletes any key from `b.Extra`, but append-only execution evidence is also stored in `b.Extra["events"]`. In a temp bead store, `go run . bead evidence add <id> ...` followed by `go run . bead update <id> --unset events` makes `ddx bead evidence list <id> --json` return `[]`, so this patch gives normal metadata updates a data-loss path that can erase audit evidence and violate the append-only contract of `ddx bead evidence`.

633:{"acceptance":"SD-016 states that cost_usd=-1 means unknown rather than known cost, direct cost aggregation excludes the sentinel value, and the cost_state / cost precedence rules align with FEAT-014 and FEAT-006 sentinel semantics","claimed-at":"2026-04-09T23:00:02Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"2091135","created_at":"2026-04-09T22:35:00.80036448Z","description":"Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-016-process-metrics.md:191
Category: correctness
Severity: high
Description: SD-016 defines `cost_state=known` whenever a session row reports `cost_usd` and says bead lifecycle cost should use `session.cost_usd` whenever it is present. But FEAT-014 and FEAT-006 explicitly define `cost_usd=-1` as the sentinel for unknown cost, not a real price. Implementing SD-016 as written would treat unknown-cost sessions as known values, producing negative dollar totals and skipping the estimation/unknown fallback for a core FEAT-016 metric.
Suggested fix: Update SD-016 so `cost_usd=-1` is mapped to `cost_state=unknown` and excluded from direct cost sums, with estimation or unknown fallback applied only to non-sentinel values.","id":"hx-72b9a368","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding","severity:high"],"owner":"erik","priority":0,"spec-id":"docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-016-process-metrics.md","status":"closed","title":"correctness: treat cost_usd=-1 as unknown in SD-016","updated_at":"2026-04-10T00:21:23.11757511Z"}

635:{"acceptance":"ddx bead show hx-010a50cf --json omits closing_commit_sha, and closing a metadata-only review bead through the supported ddx CLI no longer stamps the prior HEAD or unrelated metadata commit as closing provenance when no new close commit lands","claimed-at":"2026-04-10T10:22:57Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"2123793","created_at":"2026-04-10T09:37:30.081467159Z","description":"Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: .ddx/beads.jsonl:27
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: During this last-commit review, the supported `ddx bead close hx-010a50cf` flow closed a metadata-only `action:review` bead and persisted `closing_commit_sha=d20a24fd8ff635481f19c934e8643472ed0a7cde` on the new tracker row. That SHA is the previously reviewed provenance-repair commit, not a close commit created by the review workflow, and FEAT-019 explicitly says tracker-only backfills should omit `closing_commit_sha` rather than point replay at an unrelated metadata or earlier implementation boundary. The repo source and tests still indicate review beads should suppress this field, so the durable tracker state produced by the current supported CLI path remains misleading.
Suggested fix: Make the supported bead-close path omit `closing_commit_sha` for metadata-only review closes when no landed close commit exists, add regression coverage for that no-new-commit path, and repair hx-010a50cf so its closing provenance is absent rather than pointing at d20a24f.","id":"hx-73a3c3c0","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding","severity:medium","area:bead","area:cli"],"owner":"erik","priority":0,"spec-id":".ddx/beads.jsonl","status":"closed","title":"correctness: metadata-only review close still stamps prior HEAD provenance","updated_at":"2026-04-10T10:25:57.586690085Z"}
636:{"acceptance":"With git.auto_commit=always in a git repo and DDX_BEAD_DIR unset, running 'ddx bead close <id>' leaves 'git status --short' clean, and 'git show HEAD -- .ddx/beads.jsonl' includes the bead row with closing_commit_sha equal to 'git rev-parse HEAD' rather than requiring an extra uncommitted worktree update","claimed-at":"2026-04-09T23:35:05Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"2688734","closing_commit_sha":"c42a407f810629b99954f1258496f66e8d060635","created_at":"2026-04-09T23:33:20.048985185Z","description":"Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: cli/cmd/bead.go:518
Category: integration
Severity: medium
Description: The new close flow writes bead status first, auto-commits .ddx/beads.jsonl, and only then writes closing_commit_sha back to the store. In a clean temp git repo with git.auto_commit=always and DDX_BEAD_DIR unset, running 'ddx bead close <id>' leaves 'git status --short' dirty with ' M .ddx/beads.jsonl'. The landed HEAD commit 9494afc82408cac9a650d616fc3207197e1dfbd6 contains only the status=open->closed change, while the durable closing_commit_sha appears only in the uncommitted worktree diff. That means the command still fails to land close provenance atomically and leaves replay/audit metadata outside the recorded close commit.
Suggested fix: Restructure bead close so the recorded closing_commit_sha is present in the same landed commit that closes the bead, or perform and persist a second deterministic commit/update so git status is clean after close and HEAD contains the final provenance-bearing row.","id":"hx-73c95bab","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding","severity:medium"],"owner":"erik","parent":"hx-df089a3d","priority":0,"spec-id":"cli/cmd/bead.go","status":"closed","title":"integration: persist close provenance in the landed tracker commit","updated_at":"2026-04-09T23:42:54.706314959Z"}

File: cli/cmd/doctor.go:452
Category: integration
Severity: high
Description: `checkInstalledPlugins` iterates every row in installed state without filtering by package type, even though `InstalledEntry` records resource installs as well as plugins. A minimal repro with an installed `resource` entry (`persona/example`) makes `ddx doctor --plugins` emit `/tmp/.../persona.md/package.yaml: open ...: not a directory` and `missing package.yaml` for a non-plugin file. This breaks the plugin audit for users who have installed single resources.
Suggested fix: Restrict `ddx doctor --plugins` to package types that actually represent plugin roots (at least `plugin`, and `workflow` if workflows are intentionally audited), and skip `resource` entries entirely.","id":"hx-74a3aa15","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding","severity:high"],"owner":"erik","priority":0,"spec-id":"cli/cmd/doctor.go","status":"closed","title":"integration: limit doctor --plugins to plugin installs","updated_at":"2026-04-10T03:53:37.986281397Z"}

Category: drift
Severity: medium
Description: The new frontend-e2e job sets up Node, keys its cache from `cli/internal/server/frontend/package-lock.json`, and runs `npm ci`, `npx playwright install`, and `npm run test:e2e`. The active `typescript-bun` concern and TP-002 declare Bun as the frontend package manager and script runner, with `bun.lock` as the authoritative lockfile and `bunx`/`bun run` as the expected commands. Wiring this job to npm cements a second dependency-resolution path in CI that can drift from the declared Bun toolchain and from local/frontend build behavior.

File: cli/cmd/agent_usage.go:247
Category: integration
Severity: medium
Description: The new compatibility merge uses one global `migrationCutoff` taken from the earliest routing outcome in the log directory and then drops every legacy session row at or after that timestamp before merging (`aggregateUsageFromRoutingMetrics` + `aggregateUsageAggs`). That still under-reports mixed stores when routing metrics exist for one harness but another harness still has legacy-only rows after that cutoff. Repro with the current binary: create `routing-outcomes.jsonl` containing only a claude row at `2026-04-09T10:00:00Z`, create `sessions.jsonl` containing that mirrored claude row plus a codex legacy row at `2026-04-10T10:00:00Z`, then run `ddx agent usage --since 2026-04-01 --format json`; the output only includes claude and silently drops codex even though the log directory still contains a valid post-cutoff legacy session.
Suggested fix: Make the compatibility boundary and dedupe key harness-scoped or row-scoped rather than global, so legacy rows without a matching routing outcome remain visible until that harness/session has actually migrated; add regression coverage for a mixed directory where one harness has routing outcomes and another still only has legacy sessions after the first outcome timestamp.","id":"hx-80c953b2","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding","severity:medium","area:agent","area:cli"],"owner":"erik","priority":0,"spec-id":"cli/cmd/agent_usage.go","status":"closed","title":"integration: keep legacy-only harness rows visible during mixed usage migration","updated_at":"2026-04-10T10:35:14.704961333Z"}

Severity: medium
Description: Commit fb9bdf1 closes ddx-7e88c022 with closing_commit_sha=4563fa7, an abbreviated SHA. The close store documents this field as the exact closing commit SHA when known, but manual close evidence is persisted verbatim. Replay later resolves closing_commit_sha through git rev-parse to choose the baseline commit, so storing a short SHA weakens audit and replay determinism as the repository grows and abbreviations can become ambiguous or invalid. 672:{"acceptance":"A review bead that already carries a valid closing_commit_sha keeps that SHA after ddx bead close when no new close commit lands, while stale prior-HEAD tracker provenance is still removed for metadata-only review closes","claimed-at":"2026-04-10T10:50:03Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"2270767","closing_commit_sha":"d51f18c93d88901d833c9cc82ab280611e7690d2","created_at":"2026-04-10T10:32:22.111979389Z","description":"Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: cli/cmd/bead.go:649
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: Commit f645daf fixes the stale-review-provenance case by deleting any existing `closing_commit_sha` before a review bead is closed without `--commit`, but the new branch cannot distinguish stale provenance from a valid implementation boundary that was intentionally set earlier. Reproducing against the checked-out source with `/tmp/ddx-review` built from `cli/` and `git.auto_commit: never` shows that creating a review bead, setting `closing_commit_sha` to the real HEAD via `bead update --set`, and then running `bead close` removes the field entirely from the closed bead. That regresses FEAT-019 replay/audit semantics for review beads that already carried correct provenance, because the supported close path now erases valid history rather than only suppressing misleading tracker-only backfills.
Suggested fix: Narrow the pre-close cleanup to only stale tracker-only provenance, or preserve an existing `closing_commit_sha` unless the command can prove it points at the prior metadata-only HEAD being repaired; add regression coverage for the preserve-valid-provenance path under metadata-only review close.","id":"hx-939516c7","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding","severity:medium","area:bead","area:cli"],"owner":"erik","priority":0,"spec-id":"cli/cmd/bead.go","status":"closed","title":"correctness: preserve valid review closing provenance on metadata-only close","updated_at":"2026-04-10T10:51:40.697890368Z"}

File: cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:169
Category: integration
Severity: medium
Description: The migration switched the embedded runtime to github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/agent, but the default preset in AgentRunConfig still uses `"forge"`, and the checked-in sample config still sets `.ddx/config.yaml` to `preset: forge`. In v0.1.0, the agent library only exposes presets `agent`, `benchmark`, `minimal`, `claude`, `codex`, and `cursor`; unknown names silently fall back to `agent`. That means DDx now ignores the configured/default preset name with no warning, which makes prompt behavior implicit and leaves the runtime/config surface out of sync with the migrated library.
Suggested fix: Update the embedded default and checked-in config to a supported preset name (`agent` or another intentional choice), remove stale forge preset references from the config surface, and add a regression test that the configured default preset is one of the embedded library's supported presets rather than relying on unknown-name fallback.","id":"hx-96df1675","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding"],"owner":"erik","priority":0,"spec-id":"cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go","status":"closed","title":"integration: replace unsupported forge preset defaults after ddx-agent migration","updated_at":"2026-04-10T00:33:53.097249638Z"}

File: cli/cmd/bead.go:80
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: Commit 71f97be suppresses closing_commit_sha whenever the landed close commit touches only paths under .ddx/. That is too broad: .ddx contains real governed artifacts such as config and other tracked workspace files, not just tracker backfills. In a temp repo with git.auto_commit=always and DDX_BEAD_DIR unset, creating a bead, modifying a tracked .ddx/notes.txt file, and running ddx bead close lands a close commit that changes .ddx/beads.jsonl and .ddx/notes.txt, but the bead still omits closing_commit_sha. FEAT-019 only says to omit metadata-only tracker backfills; dropping provenance for legitimate .ddx-scoped work makes replay/audit fall back to sessionless mode even though the implementation boundary is available.
Suggested fix: Detect metadata-only close commits more narrowly, for example by distinguishing pure .ddx/beads.jsonl backfills from other tracked .ddx artifacts, and add regression coverage for a close commit that lands tracked .ddx work.","id":"hx-98fec407","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding","severity:medium"],"owner":"erik","priority":0,"spec-id":"cli/cmd/bead.go","status":"closed","title":"correctness: preserve closing provenance for legitimate .ddx-only work","updated_at":"2026-04-10T00:48:20.400206217Z"}

File: cli/cmd/agent_usage.go:201
Category: integration
Severity: medium
Description: Commit 5349a98 removes the per-harness cutoff and now suppresses legacy sessions.jsonl rows only when usageMirrorKey finds a matching native_session_id, trace_id, or span_id (aggregateUsageAggs lines 201-207 and aggregateUsageFromRoutingMetrics lines 246-270). Current external-harness writes do not guarantee any of those identifiers: both the session row and routing outcome copy result.AgentSessionID, then only fill correlation IDs when present (cli/internal/agent/runner.go:722-753, cli/internal/agent/routing_metrics.go:231-256). For normal codex/claude-style runs without correlation metadata, both records remain unkeyed, so ddx agent usage now counts the same live run twice. Reproduced with the shipped CLI by creating one codex sessions.jsonl row and one matching routing-outcomes.jsonl row with identical tokens/cost but no mirror IDs; ddx agent usage --since 2026-04-01 --format json reported sessions=2, input_tokens=400, output_tokens=40, cost_usd=5 instead of one session.
Suggested fix: Preserve a compatibility boundary for unkeyed current rows, or emit a stable mirror identifier for every routing outcome/session pair before relying solely on identity-match dedupe; add regression coverage for a mixed current-format session/outcome pair that lacks native_session_id, trace_id, and span_id.","id":"hx-a30d162c","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding","severity:medium","area:agent","area:cli"],"owner":"erik","priority":0,"spec-id":"cli/cmd/agent_usage.go","status":"closed","title":"integration: avoid double-counting current sessions without mirror IDs in agent usage","updated_at":"2026-04-10T13:47:33.514964363Z"}

File: cli/cmd/bead.go:567
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: Commit 1bc26181bae1e88cd6c29fc11f20c5904ed2ae8a normalizes `ddx bead close --commit` through `git rev-parse` before persisting the value. That fixes short SHAs inside a git repo, but it also makes manual close provenance unusable in non-git workspaces where bead commands otherwise still work. Reproduced with the built CLI in a temp directory outside git: `ddx bead create ...` succeeds, then `ddx bead close <id> --commit 1234567890abcdef1234567890abcdef12345678` now fails with `git rev-parse ... exit status 128`. Before this change, the command accepted an exact commit SHA verbatim. That is a behavior regression for portable/manual tracker workflows and contradicts the command contract that `--commit` records the exact closing commit SHA when known.
Suggested fix: Normalize only when the supplied value is an abbreviated ref in a git repository, but preserve already-full commit SHAs verbatim when git resolution is unavailable; add regression coverage for `bead close --commit <40-char-sha>` in a non-git workspace.","id":"hx-a77820e4","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding","severity:medium"],"owner":"erik","priority":0,"spec-id":"cli/cmd/bead.go","status":"closed","title":"correctness: preserve manual close provenance in non-git workspaces","updated_at":"2026-04-10T05:38:51.46880358Z"}

700:{"acceptance":"A correlated session row with model=gpt-5.4 and cost_usd=0 is reported with cost_state=known and cost_usd=0 in both Cost and Summary outputs, cost_usd=-1 remains unknown, and cd cli && go test ./internal/processmetrics -count=1 passes","claimed-at":"2026-04-10T11:04:48Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"2328880","closing_commit_sha":"0dcccfc676a18d03fbfdf8879fb27231f03532c2","created_at":"2026-04-10T09:25:39.76617496Z","description":"Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: cli/internal/processmetrics/processmetrics.go:1084
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: Commit 58d6229309445ce3e571b85e14f1ba423b2198b1 still treats an explicit session row with cost_usd=0 as estimated whenever the model has non-zero pricing. Both sessionCostState and sessionDerivedCost continue to special-case only sess.CostUSD > 0, so a priced-model row such as model=gpt-5.4 with cost_usd=0 falls through to sessionEstimatedCost and is reported as cost_state=estimated with a non-zero derived cost instead of preserving the recorded zero. The new regression test only covers qwen/qwen3-coder-30b, whose estimator also returns 0, so it misses the case that actually matters for explicit zero-cost rows on priced models.
Suggested fix: Treat any explicit cost_usd >= 0 as the authoritative known cost before consulting estimator fallback, and add regression coverage using a priced model plus the -1 sentinel so both known-zero and unknown paths are locked down.","id":"hx-aa099c77","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding","severity:medium","area:cli","area:api"],"owner":"erik","priority":0,"spec-id":"cli/internal/processmetrics/processmetrics.go","status":"closed","title":"correctness: explicit zero-cost rows must override estimator output","updated_at":"2026-04-10T11:06:54.708493046Z"}

Pass 1 correctness: API-001 duplicates post-run and land/preserve ownership around Unified interface for dispatching work to AI coding agents.

Supports multiple harnesses (codex, claude, gemini, etc.) with output capture,
token tracking, session logging, and multi-agent quorum.

Examples:
  ddx agent run --harness codex --prompt task.md
  ddx agent run --quorum majority --harnesses codex,claude --prompt task.md
  ddx agent list
  ddx agent capabilities codex
  ddx agent doctor
  ddx agent log

Usage:
  ddx agent [flags]
  ddx agent [command]

Available Commands:
  benchmark    Run a benchmark suite comparing agent harnesses
  capabilities Show agent model and reasoning-level capabilities
  condense     Filter raw agent output to progress-relevant lines
  doctor       Check agent harness health
  list         List available agent harnesses
  log          Show agent session history
  replay       Replay a bead with a different agent harness or model
  run          Invoke an agent with a prompt
  usage        Show per-harness token and cost summary

Flags:
  -h, --help   help for agent

Global Flags:
